version: 2026 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report — Gen AI section
description: FINRA's 2026 guidance mandates governance over AI agents used by broker-dealers and financial services firms — pre-approval of AI use cases, human-in-the-loop validation of client-impacting actions, tamper-evident audit trails, supervisory review, and record retention. SidClaw's four primitives (Identity, Policy, Approval, Trace) map directly to these requirements, and extend supervisory controls already required by FINRA Rules 3110 (supervision), 3130 (CEO certification of supervisory controls), and 4511 (books and records).

mapping.title: Pre-approval of AI use cases with documented sign-offs and supervisory owners
sidclaw_features: Agent Registry (Identity primitive) — every agent has a named owner, team, environment, authority model, and autonomy tier
Authority model on each agent (self / delegated / hybrid) documents the scope of delegation
Lifecycle state (active / suspended / revoked) — supervisors can revoke an agent immediately
Policy rules require rationale and change_summary on every version — documented supervisory approval chain

mapping.title: Human-in-the-loop validation on client-impacting or material decisions
sidclaw_features: Approval primitive — policies with effect 'approval_required' pause execution until a reviewer decides
Context-rich approval cards — reviewers see operation, target integration, data classification, risk, agent reasoning
Separation-of-duties check — an agent's owner cannot approve that agent's own request
Reviewer decision notes are captured as part of the permanent audit trail

mapping.title: Audit trail of agent actions, decisions, and supervisory overrides
sidclaw_features: Hash-chained audit traces (SHA-256) — tamper-evident, independently verifiable
Every evaluation generates a correlated event chain: initiation, identity resolution, policy match, approval, outcome
Trace verification endpoint — GET /api/v1/traces/:id/verify
Policy version is recorded on every evaluation so reviewers can reconstruct exactly what rule was in force at decision time

mapping.title: Guardrails that constrain agent behavior within declared authority
sidclaw_features: Policy engine with priority-ordered, first-match, secure-by-default-deny semantics
Data classification hierarchy (public / internal / confidential / restricted) enforced per action
Risk classification on every approval (low / medium / high / critical)
Rate-limit, time-restriction, and cost-threshold policy conditions cap blast radius

mapping.title: Supervisory review aligned to FINRA Rule 3110 (supervision)
sidclaw_features: RBAC with three roles (admin, reviewer, viewer) — reviewers perform the supervisory check
Pending-approvals queue on the overview dashboard surfaces items awaiting supervisory decision
Webhook events (approval.requested, policy.violation) alert supervisors promptly
Separation of duties enforces independent supervisory review

mapping.title: Evidence supporting FINRA Rule 3130 annual supervisory control certification
sidclaw_features: Exportable audit evidence bundle (JSON / CSV) for the CEO's annual 3130 certification
Policy version history demonstrates that supervisory controls were tested and documented
Trace integrity verification provides cryptographic assurance to the certifying officer
gaps: The 3130 annual report and testing methodology remain the firm's responsibility — SidClaw provides evidence but does not author the certification

mapping.title: Record retention aligned to FINRA Rule 4511 (general books and records)
sidclaw_features: Trace retention configurable per plan (7 / 30 / 90 days; enterprise custom, supporting multi-year retention)
Immutable hash-chained events prevent covert modification of retained records
JSON and CSV export for long-term archival in WORM-compliant storage
SIEM export for continuous forwarding to regulated retention systems
gaps: Rule 4511 commonly requires 3-6 year WORM retention; customer must configure export to a compliant storage tier and retain beyond SidClaw's native retention window

references: FINRA 2026 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report — Gen AI section (https://www.finra.org/rules-guidance/guidance/reports/2026-finra-annual-regulatory-oversight-report/gen-ai)
FINRA Rule 3130 — Annual Certification of Compliance and Supervisory Processes
FINRA Rule 4511 — General Requirements for Books and Records
